channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Heavy Israeli bombardment in northern Gaza as UN peacekeepers in Lebanon are hit again
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Palestinians in northern Gaza described heavy Israeli bombardment Saturday in the hours after airstrikes killed at least 22 people, as Israel warned people there and in southern Lebanon to get out of the way of offensives against the Hamas and Hezbollah militant groups.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinian officials say 22 killed in Israeli airstrikes
item.description: The Israeli military on Saturday renewed its orders for Palestinians in the northern Gaza Strip to leave their homes and shelters as troops continue a weeklong offensive against militants.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-Scottish First Minister Alex Salmond, who nearly got Scotland's independence from UK, dies at 69
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Alex Salmond, the former first minister of Scotland who for decades championed Scotland’s independence from the U.K. and nearly accomplished it, has died. He was 69.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Poland's leader plans to suspend the right to asylum as country faces pressure on Belarus border
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Poland's leader said Saturday that he plans to temporarily suspend the right to asylum as part of a new migration policy, pointing to its alleged abuse by eastern neighbor Belarus and Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hindus in Bangladesh celebrate their largest festival under tight security following attacks
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Schoolteacher Supriya Sarker is glad to celebrate Bangladesh's largest Hindu festival of Durga Puja but feels the festivities would be more jubilant without the fear and violence that overshadow this year's event.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM and Al EMRUN GARJON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK leader Keir Starmer is marking 100 days in office. It has been a rocky ride
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer marks 100 days in office on Saturday with little cause for celebration.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Drought is parching the world's largest man-made lake, stripping Zambia of its electricity
item.description: Zambia’s worst electricity blackouts in memory have been caused by a severe drought in the region that has left the critical Kariba dam with insufficient water to run its hydroelectric turbines.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Zimba, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Navy SEALs' drownings in Iranian op were preventable, investigation finds
item.description: An investigation into the death of two Navy SEALS who drowned during a mission to seize Iranian missile parts bound for the Houthi rebels in Yemen found that the deaths could have been prevented.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: U.S. expands sanctions on Iran in response to its ballistic attack on Israel
item.description: The U.S. on Friday announced new sanctions on Iran’s energy sector in response to its October 1 attack on Israel, firing roughly 180 missiles into the country.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UN peacekeepers in southern Lebanon in crosshairs of Israel’s war on Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israeli airstrikes hit the headquarters of the U.N. peacekeeping force and other positions in southern Lebanon in recent days, drawing widespread condemnation from around the world.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Boris Johnson says Biden told him his family origins 'were not really Irish at all'
item.description: Boris Johnson, former United Kingdom prime minister, said President Biden told him he was not Irish, according to his new memoir "Unleashed," which was published Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize given to Japanese organization Nihon Hidankyo for its work against nuclear weapons
item.description: The Nobel Peace Prize was awarded Friday to Nihon Hidankyo, a Japanese organization of survivors of the U.S. atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, for its activism against nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CORDER and ELENA BECATOROS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mount Everest remains believed to be climber who vanished 100 years ago
item.description: A National Geographic documentary crew has found the possible partial remains of Andrew Irvine, a British climber who vanished on Mount Everest 100 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Activists risk their lives to rescue animals in areas of Lebanon hit by Israeli airstrikes
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hours after an Israeli strike destroyed a three-story building in Beirut, killing at least 10 people, Maggie Sharawi received a telephone call from a person living nearby saying that the attack had killed a cat that had several kittens.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stellantis CEO under fire from Italian lawmakers as the group grapples with financial troubles
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italian lawmakers on Friday questioned the head of Stellantis, the world's fourth largest automaker, over the group’s strategy, one day after it announced a management shakeup in an effort to revive sales.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New arrests revive concern that nonviolent political protesters in Myanmar may be tortured
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Concern was rising on Friday that two nonviolent activists opposed to military rule in Myanmar are at high risk of torture after being arrested in raids this week in Yangon, the country’s biggest city.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy appeals to allies to keep up aid as Germany pledges new weapons package
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday it's important that allies' aid to Ukraine doesn't decrease next year as he received a pledge of a new weapons package from German Chancellor Olaf Scholz on a European tour meant to win backing for his “victory plan” aimed at ending the war wit
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea accuses South Korea of flying drones to its capital and threatens to attack next time
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea has accused rival South Korea of flying drones to its capital to drop anti-North Korean propaganda leaflets and threatened to respond with force if such flights occur again. South Korea issued a vague denial of the allegation.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkish citizens fleeing Lebanon mourn the homes and family left behind
item.description: ON BOARD THE TCG SANCAKTAR (AP) — Eyup Sabri Kirgiz gathered up his loved ones — both family and pets — and with a heavy heart left his beloved city of Beirut behind, after two weeks of deadly airstrikes that had traumatized his family.
item.dc:creator: MEHMET GUZEL and SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Partial remains of British climber believed found 100 years after Everest ascent
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Climbers believe they have found the partial remains of a British mountaineer who might — or might not — have been one of the first two people to climb Mount Everest, a century after their attempt on the world's highest peak, according to an expedition led by National Geographic.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia launches 4th aerial attack in a week against Ukraine's grain-exporting Odesa region
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A nighttime Russian missile strike on Odesa killed at least four people including a 16-year-old girl, regional authorities said Friday, in the latest in a series of attacks this week on the southern Ukrainian region that are likely intended to disrupt the country’s grain exports
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iran's national airliner accused of flying weapons to Beirut airport for terror proxy Hezbollah: Report
item.description: Iran’s domestic airline, already sanctioned for support to Russia, is being accused of aiding Hezbollah by an Iranian dissident group who has called for additional sanctions to be put on the carrier.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gender of female captain not to blame for sinking of $61M navy ship: New Zealand defense minister
item.description: New Zealand's Defense Minister has rebukes claims that the appointment of a female to captain a $61 million navy ship ultimately led to its sinking.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris faces new battles with expanding Middle East conflict
item.description: Vice President Harris is struggling to confront a rapidly worsening crisis in the Middle East as she seeks to balance unwavering support for Israel with appeals to progressive and Arab American communities.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: US Navy looks to robots, AI to extend vessel health and maintenance
item.description: Gecko Robotics uses units that observe and record millions of data points, dramatically increasing the amount of data the U.S. Navy can accumulate regarding vessel health.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 22 killed in airstrikes in central Beirut, with Israel also firing on UN peacekeepers
item.description: The air raid on central Beirut — the deadliest in over a year of war — apparently targeted two residential buildings in separate neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: BILAL HUSSEIN, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Dozens wounded, at least 22 killed in airstrikes in Beirut as tensions escalate in Israel conflict with Iran
item.description: At least 22 people were killed and dozens of others left wounded in airstrikes in central Beirut, further escalating Israel’s deadly conflict with Iran-backed Hezbollah militants in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: British YouTuber and rapper Yung Filly is charged with raping and choking a woman in Australia
item.description: PERTH, Australia (AP) — A British YouTuber and rapper known as Yung Filly has been charged with raping and choking a woman in a hotel room following an Australian music performance.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump-Putin ties face fresh scrutiny with Woodward book
item.description: Less than a month before the U.S. presidential election, former President Trump is facing fresh scrutiny on his relationship with Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Canadian woman charged with killing 3 people in 3 days, labeled a serial killer
item.description: A Canadian woman allegedly killed three people in separate incidents over three days in and around Toronto, authorities said last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Susan Rice: Trump's reported calls with Putin appear illegal
item.description: Former White House adviser and United Nations ambassador Susan Rice called out former President Trump over a report that he has spoken with Russian President Vladimir Putin at least seven times since leaving office, suggesting such calls would violate the Logan Act.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Google loses final EU court appeal against 2.4 billion euro fine in antitrust shopping case
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Google lost its final legal challenge on Tuesday against a European Union penalty for giving its own shopping recommendations an illegal advantage over rivals in search results, ending a long-running antitrust case that came with a whopping fine.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: British doctor admits to attempted murder after injecting mother’s partner with poison disguised as vaccine
item.description: British doctor, Thomas Kwan, pleaded guilty to an attempted murder charge after 'overwhelming' evidence showed he administer a poisonous fake COVID vaccination.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk's X to be reinstated in Brazil after complying with Supreme Court demands
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — The Brazilian Supreme Court's Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Tuesday authorized the restoration of social media platform X´s service in Brazil, over a month after its nationwide shutdown, according to a court document that was made public. Elon Musk’s X was blocked on Aug.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Suspect in Madeleine McCann disappearance case acquitted of unrelated sexual offense charges
item.description: A German court has acquitted Christian Brueckner, who is also under investigation in the disappearance of British toddler Madeleine McCann, in a trial over unrelated sexual offenses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea’s Kim Jong Un pushes goal to become nuclear powerhouse in warning to South Korea
item.description: Kim Jong Un issued his second ominous message, saying he plans to make Pyongyang a top nuclear player and expand its capabilities in face of South Korea-U.S. partnership.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexican mayor murdered less than a week after taking office
item.description: Mayor Alejandro Arcos of Chilpancingo, Mexico, has been confirmed dead less than a week after being sworn into the notoriously dangerous city's highest office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
